item.title: Rickenbacker "4001" 1979 — $2,693.39
item.description:
$2,693.39 · Very Good · Qsic - secondhand musical instruments store from Japan -

Welcome to Qsic's Reverb Shop!
1. The description texts have been generated from our Japanese site by automatic translation.
Instrument or music terms are often not translated correctly, so please let us know if you have any questions.

2. For orders from countries or regions not covered by EMS or FedEx, the difference in shipping costs will be billed again via PayPal after your order is placed.
At first, please feel free to order as it is.

Product Details

A Rickenbacker 4001, a representative bass model, has arrived.

This particular instrument, with serial number "SA 230," is believed to have been manufactured in January 1979, although the pot date indicates 1978.

Its maple body, maple neck-through construction, and rosewood fingerboard contribute to a tight and fast tone. With its characteristic Rickenbacker hard attack and clear sound, it stands out even in a band ensemble.

The front pickup has been replaced with a Seymour Duncan, and the wiring has been modified, resulting in a standard mono output.

The reddish finish, which allows the wood grain to show through, combined with the aged appearance and what appears to be a Burgundyglo-like color, gives it a vintage feel.

[Specifications]
Body: Maple
Neck: Maple / Neck-Through
Fingerboard: Rosewood
Nut Width: 41.3mm
Scale: 33.25inch
Frets: 20F
String Spacing: Approx. 18mm
Pickups: Seymour Duncan Front Pickup / Rickenbacker Hi-Gain Type Bridge Pickup
Preamp: None
Control: 2 Volume, 2 Tone, 3-Way Selector
Color: Burgundyglo

[Condition]
Discoloration and rust on parts due to age. Large dents near the 5th and 8th frets on the neck grip.
The strap pins have been replaced with Schaller type pins; the strap-side parts are not included. There are mounting holes on the end pin side due to the replacement. Also, the "treble tone" marker is missing.
The front pickup has been replaced with a Seymour Duncan.
Due to wiring changes, it is not connected to the stereo output of the Rick O'Sound and is currently a standard mono output.
There are signs of use and modifications commensurate with its age.

Fret life: Approximately 70%
Truss rod: Plenty of adjustment range
Electronics: No problems
Weight: 4.39kg
Neck: No problems

[Adjustments performed]
Overall cleaning
Neck adjustment
String height adjustment (2.7mm-2.2mm at 12th fret)
Octave adjustment
Serial number: SA 230
Our internal reference number: QM652

[Accessories]
Generic hard case (black)

-----------------------------------
Please note that all items are for sale in our Store in Kobe.
There is a chance they might be sold out.

All items are shipped tracked and insured.
All import duties or customs fees are the buyers responsibility - please be aware of the requirements for your country.
If you have any questions please feel free to ask.

item.description:

Product Details

The LBA-PJ is a PJ-style electric bass guitar from Laid Back.

While retaining the ease of use of traditional basses, its slim body design and thinner neck make it a well-balanced instrument that's easy for beginners to pick up.

The body is made of poplar, the neck is maple, and the fingerboard is made of high-density engineered wood.

It features an 864mm scale length, 40.0mm nut width, 22 frets, and is equipped with both P-Style and J-Style pickups.

The simple 2-volume, 1-tone control configuration allows for intuitive sound shaping.

When plugged into an amplifier, the P-Style pickups provide a thick, cohesive bass tone, while the J-Style pickups allow for adjustment of definition and clarity.

The attack response is responsive, making it easy to create a rounded foundation when fingerpicking, and the attack of phrases is clearly defined when using a pick.

This instrument boasts a versatile PJ configuration suitable for a wide range of genres and a size designed for ease of use.

This model is perfect for beginners just starting out on bass, as well as those looking for a casual, versatile backup instrument.

Please consider this opportunity!

[Specifications]
Body: Poplar
Neck: Maple
Fingerboard: High-density engineered wood
Nut Width: 40.0 mm
Scale Length: 864 mm
Number of Frets: 22 F
Pickups: P-Style, J-Style
Controls: 2 Volume, 1 Tone

[Condition]

There are no noticeable dents, scuffs, or scratches on the body surface or around the neck, and the metal parts show no significant tarnishing.
It shows minimal signs of use and is in a condition that will appeal to those who prioritize appearance. There are no other particularly noticeable major scratches.

Product Details

The BPB-1DX RSM/M is a PB-style electric bass guitar from the Bacchus Universe Series.

While maintaining a traditional look, it features a roasted maple neck and fingerboard, balancing ease of use with modern practicality.

The body is made of poplar, while the neck and fingerboard are made of roasted maple.

With a 34-inch scale, 38mm nut width, 305R fingerboard radius, and 21 frets, it boasts a simple P-style configuration while maintaining excellent handling.

The pickups are Original P-Type, and the controls are intuitive with one volume and one tone.

When plugged into an amplifier, it delivers a solid bass tone characteristic of a Precision Bass, enhanced by the clarity and definition of the roasted maple fingerboard.

The attack is well-defined, the sound has a natural rise, and it's easy to grasp the contours of phrases whether fingerpicking or using a pick. The easy-to-handle output of the Original P-Type pickups is also a major appeal.

This bass combines simple controls and the reassuring feel of a PB-style bass with the practicality of roasted maple and DX specifications.

It's an easy-to-use bass, perfect as a first instrument, a backup, or for home recording.

Please consider it!

item.title: Gibson SG Standard Bass Silver Sparkle — $1,146.95
item.description:
$1,146.95 · Very Good · Qsic - secondhand musical instruments store from Japan -

Welcome to Qsic's Reverb Shop!
1. The description texts have been generated from our Japanese site by automatic translation.
Instrument or music terms are often not translated correctly, so please let us know if you have any questions.

2. For orders from countries or regions not covered by EMS or FedEx, the difference in shipping costs will be billed again via PayPal after your order is placed.
At first, please feel free to order as it is.

Product Details

The SG Standard Bass Silver Sparkle is a limited-edition color model that perfectly recreates a unique bass guitar based on a historical Gibson model beloved by many renowned players, incorporating the specific preferences of Koki Matoba from the TV anime "Argonavis from BanG Dream!".

It features a mahogany body and neck, a rosewood fingerboard, and a 12-inch radius fingerboard with 20 frets. It has a Rhythm SG Bass pickup on the neck side and a Lead SG Bass pickup on the bridge side, allowing for intuitive volume balance adjustment with two volume controls and a master tone control. While simple, it offers an easy-to-handle configuration that delivers the characteristic punch of an SG bass.

When plugged into an amplifier, the short-scale bass delivers a quick, punchy low end, with a forward-projecting core sound. Fingerstyle playing produces a rounded, thick tone with sustain, while pick playing adds a defined attack. The sound is well-balanced without being overly expansive, making it easy to use for everything from rock riffs to vocal-supporting lines.

This model, combining the classic SG shape, striking colors, and easy-to-handle scale length, is ideal for those who want to comfortably produce low-pitched bass lines. The dazzling Silver Sparkle finish also makes it a great choice for those seeking a strong stage presence.

Please consider this opportunity.

[Specifications]
Body: Mahogany
Neck: Mahogany
Fingerboard: Rosewood
Fingerboard Radius: 12inch-R
Nut Width: 40.6mm
Scale Length: 30.5inch
Number of Frets: 20F
Pickups: (Neck) Rhythm SG Bass (Bridge) Lead SG Bass
Controls: 2 Volumes & 1 Master Tone

[Condition]
There is a noticeable dent near the 4th fret, and approximately four screw holes that appear to be for attaching a finger rest. The headstock shows paint chips due to dents, and there are raised paint areas around the rear pickup that appear to be repair marks. Otherwise, all functions work correctly. Due to the multiple signs of use, including paint chips and raised areas, this is a great bargain for those who don't mind the appearance.

Product Details

Spirit by Steinberger XT-2

The "XT-2" from Spirit, a cost-effective brand that offers the classic Steinberger headless style at an affordable price, has arrived!

Its compact size makes it comfortable to play, and its ease of switching between instruments and light handling are its main attractions. While possessing a unique presence, it's a model that can be enjoyed from everyday practice to studio sessions!

This instrument features a basswood body, maple neck, rosewood fingerboard, and 24 frets. The controls are easy to use, with separate volume controls on the neck and bridge sides, plus a master tone control. Its simplicity and ease of adjustment are also key features!

The sound, due to its smaller body size, has a clear attack, maintaining a solid low-end while avoiding muddiness. It responds directly to picking, making it easy to bring out nuances in phrases and rhythms, and it naturally handles tight lines.

With two humbuckers, it easily produces a thick, punchy sound that stands out in an ensemble, and even on clean tones, it maintains a strong presence with a crisp yet warm tone.

While possessing an interesting, unconventional shape, this is a guitar we recommend for those looking for a portable and easy-to-handle instrument!

item.description:

Product Details

The Fender American Vintage II 1954 Precision Bass is an electric bass guitar crafted with specifications inspired by the 1954 Precision Bass.

It features a Vintage Blonde ash body paired with a maple neck, offering a simple yet powerful look reminiscent of the early models.

It boasts an ash body, one-piece maple neck, and maple fingerboard construction, with a 34-inch scale, 43.2mm nut width, and 20 frets.

Equipped with a single Pure Vintage '54 Single-Coil Precision pickup, it features a simple control scheme with only master volume and master tone controls.

The passive design allows for intuitive sound shaping, and the 19mm string spacing provides ample room for right-hand control.

When plugged into an amplifier, it delivers a solid low end and a clearly defined attack.

It offers the fast response characteristic of a single-coil pickup; fingerstyle playing produces a clear attack, while pick playing delivers an even more powerful tone.

With minimal added embellishments, it's easy to control the tone and nuances of touch, resulting in a natural and cohesive sound from root-supporting basslines to dynamic phrases.

This instrument is perfect for those seeking the look and straightforward sound characteristic of early Precision Basses.

It's not only a collector's item, but also an attractive instrument to actually play.

Please consider this opportunity.

item.title: 【new】moon / JB-5A N/E CR 4.260kg #59441【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $4,173
item.description:
$4,173 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Moon is a leading, long-established guitar brand in Japan.

Highly regarded by many professional artists, Larry Graham and Mitsuru Sudo are known to be fans of the brand. (Titles omitted)

This model is a JB model equipped with the active circuitry that they excel at.

The body is made of ash and the fretboard is ebony, a configuration typical of Moon.

The pickup is the newly adopted H.Y.G JB-5.

It's a simple single-coil pickup with complete hand-wiring.

The preamp is the classic Bartolini XTCT.

As is typical of Moon, the ultra-smooth playability is the greatest feature of this model, providing an excellent playing feel for both fingerstyle and slap bass.

The bouncy, responsive feel is impressive, making this bass a joy to play.

If you're looking for a high-quality 5-string JB type, don't miss this opportunity.

SPECS

Body: Ash 3p
Parts: Chrome
Neck: Maple
Color: Natural
Fingerboard: Ebony
Pickups: H.Y.G JB5 & XTCT
etc. Urethane Finish

Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: Original gig bag, warranty card, wrench

Text: Ryoma Terao

Inspection 담당: Higashie

Product Photos: Higashie

Manufacturer's warranty included

◯ About GIB and TONIQ warranties

● Lifetime free maintenance

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free "string vibration setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

・Please present your warranty card with your name filled in.

・Only applicable to the person who purchased the instrument at a GIB or TONIQ physical store or online shop.

・Please bear the cost of shipping and other miscellaneous expenses.

● Guaranteed Buyback Price

If you sell your purchased item within 6 months of purchase, we guarantee a buyback price. The price is 50% for new items and 70% for used items.

● Warranty Against Defects

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. Excluding some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.

The warranty period is 3 months for used items and 1 year for new items.

Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:

* Malfunctions and damage caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.

* Replacement of consumables.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual.

* Malfunctions or damage to parts repaired or modified by parties other than our company.

* If the warranty card does not have your name, model name, serial number, etc. filled in. Or, if the wording has been altered.

* If the warranty certificate is not presented or is lost.

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan.)

● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in

When you purchase an item, you will receive a 10% discount on the sales price when you use our trade-in service. (Consignment items are excluded. This discount applies only if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the sales price.)

Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.

We ship with sufficient packaging.

We also ship internationally.

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

Product Details

The Black Smoker STD BETA J5 BS XL is a 5-string JB-style bass from the Japanese brand Black Smoker.

It features a 34.5-inch scale length, typical of XL models, making it easy to maintain clarity down to the low B.

This instrument offers the ease of use characteristic of passive basses and a practical range suitable for live performance.

It boasts a classic combination of an alder body, maple neck, and Indian rosewood fingerboard, with a 48mm nut width, 18mm string spacing, and 21 frets.

It's equipped with two Black Smoker Original Big Single pickups, with a mini-switch for series/parallel switching.

Despite its simple volume and tone control configuration, it's easy to shape the sound to suit different situations.

When plugged into an amplifier, it has the thick low end characteristic of a 5-string bass, while also exhibiting a clear attack and easy pitch control.

In parallel mode, it delivers a tight, clear tone, while in series mode, it provides a powerful, full sound, making it versatile for fingerstyle and slap bass, maintaining its definition throughout.

This bass is ideal for those who want a passive 5-string as their main instrument, or for anyone looking for a reliable JB-style bass they can use without hesitation in live settings.

With its simple configuration, it offers stable low B tones and a wide range of tonal options, making it a dependable instrument.

Product Details

FUJIGEN (FgN) JMJ2-ASH-MSBB02

The JMJ (J-Standard Mighty Jazz), a flagship model in the J-Standard series, is a core jazz bass type model in the FGN bass lineup.

The 2021 renewal retains the powerful sound of its predecessor while evolving with specifications that emphasize a more stable low-end.

This model features a beautiful look with an ash body, maple fingerboard, and see-through blue burst color.

In addition to the bright and prominent sound unique to ash wood, it features J-Standard fret edge processing for improved playability and smooth fingering.

The neck has a comfortable shape similar to a jazz bass type, achieving a balanced playing feel.

The bridge is equipped with FGN's original 2-way design, supporting both standard string-through and string-through-body stringing.

The pickups are Seymour Duncan Quarter Pound and SMB-4D, with a J-type in the neck position and an MM-type in the bridge position, allowing for a wide range of tonal possibilities. Furthermore, the push-pull switch on the tone pot allows for coil tapping of the rear pickup, enabling a range of tones from the thick, sustained sound characteristic of MM-type pickups to the crisp, articulate sound of JB-type pickups.

Please consider this opportunity.

item.title: 1992 Sandberg S1 Flamed Koa - Natural | Vintage German Boutique Custom 4-String Electric Bass Neck Through Active Electronics Bartolini Pickups 24 Frets 3.88kg | Case — $2,384.20
item.description:
$2,384.20 · Very Good · Simon's Gear Garage

I am offering a Sandberg S1 Koa from 1992, a handcrafted in Braunschweig, Lower Saxony (Germany), vintage 4-string electric bass by the premium bass forge Sandberg, featuring a solid mahogany body (weighing just 3.88kg!), wild-figured Hawaiian koa top, four times mahogany-reinforced Canadian hardrock maple neck in neck-through construction with “C” neck profile and 39.5mm brass nut, fine rosewood fingerboard with elaborate mother-of-pearl dot inlays, two tonally versatile Bartolini soapbar pickups, sophisticated active electronics with Sandberg 3-band EQ and push/ pull treble frequency switch, long 864mm scale length, sealed Schaller tuners, and adjustable Sandberg bridge in an authentically aged natural high-gloss finish with gold hardware in very good condition, including black SKB Freedom hardshell case and professional setup with 50-105 Ernie Ball strings.



Since the mid-1980s, Holger Stonjek’s team in Braunschweig has been building high-quality basses with innovative features and exceptional craftsmanship. No wonder, then, that Sandberg is now one of the most popular European bass manufacturers!


The S1 is one of Sandberg’s early high-end models and exemplifies the craftsmanship and high standards of the then-young German bass manufacturer in the early 1990s. In particular, the combination of a wildly flamed koa top and the neck though construction gives this instrument a unique boutique character that is rarely found in Sandberg’s current lineup.


Plugged in, the bass delivers an extremely balanced and punchy foundation. The Bartolini soapbar pickups provide a warm, rich fundamental tone with clearly defined mids and pleasant highs. In combination with the active 3-band electronics, the sound can be shaped with great precision, allowing you to coax everything from transparent, modern tones to powerful, assertive rock and fusion sounds.


The four-piece mahogany-reinforced canadian hardrock maple neck in a neck-through construction offers particularly efficient tone transmission with long sustain and consistent response across the entire fretboard. The comfortable “C” profile and 14” radius ensure very comfortable playability, while the 24 frets provide a wide tonal range.


The high-quality Sandberg Adjustable Bridge and Schaller tuners ensure precise adjustability and excellent tuning stability. The gold-plated hardware further underscores the high-quality character of this instrument.


Visually, the bass impresses with its elegant and timeless appearance: The striking koa top with its lively grain makes each instrument unique and harmonizes perfectly with the natural high-gloss finish and the gold hardware elements.



Technical Specifications:

  • Origin: Braunschweig, Germany
  • Year of Manufacture: 1992
  • Model: S1 4-String
  • Body: Solid mahogany
  • Top: Solid flamed Hawaiian koa
  • Neck: Four times mahogany reinforced Canadian hardrock maple neck, neck-through
  • Neck profile: “C”
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood with mother-of-pearl dot inlays
  • Fingerboard radius: 14"
  • Frets: 24
  • Nut: 39.5mm, brass (height-adjustable)
  • Headstock: Matching headstock with flamed koa veneer
  • Pickups: 2x Bartolini soapbar
  • Electronics: Sandberg 3-band EQ; 1x vol., 1x bal., 1x Treble, 1x Mid., and 1x Bass control (incl. push/pull in the treble knob for shifting the frequency range); active
  • Tuners: Sealed Schaller Bass Gold tuners
  • Bridge: Sandberg Adjustable Bridge
  • Hardware: Gold
  • Finish: Natural, high-gloss
  • Scale length: 864mm (Long Scale)
  • Weight: 3.88kg
  • Serial No.: 1091186
  • Includes black SKB Freedom hardshell case



The Sandberg S1 was only manufactured for a short time in the 1990s, and these early Sandberg basses still boast exceptional build quality.

This Sandberg S1 Koa is in very good condition and technically flawless. Visually, there is only occasional fine age-related wear, which contributes to an authentic vintage patina (see photos). The frets are in top condition.


The instrument has received a professional setup with 50-105 Ernie Ball strings, and I (a long-time guitar and bass teacher) will ship it fully insured with DHL (including bulky item surcharge) for €69.99 within Germany or €99.99 within the EU. Worldwide shipping available—individual shipping costs upon request.


All images shown are of the actual instrument for sale.

item.title: 1981 Kramer Stagemaster Special Bass White with Aluminum Neck & Hardshell Case — $1,299.99
item.description:
$1,299.99 · Very Good · hearandwear

The serial number is “A7169” and two of the pots date to 1981. The body is in very good condition overall but has some light marks and small dings (mostly around the edges). Nothing out of the ordinary for a vintage guitar and no cracks, modifications or structural issues.

The neck is straight and the frets show only light to average wear. It’s in excellent playing condition and has no major issues. The fretboard, back of the neck and headstock are clean and there are no dings, worn areas, cracks, repairs or extra holes. The nut is 1.58” wide, the neck measures 0.84” thick at the 1st fret and 0.97” thick at the 12th fret. The only issue is the “A” tuner is loose and the nut is stripped. So it can’t be tightened all the way down but it stays in tune as it should.

The pickups and electronics are in excellent working condition. It has the original Jazz Bass pickup in the bridge position and the P-Bass set has been changed to DiMarzio Will Power models. The original set had a very low ohm reading so I don’t know if they’re good or not. They are included in the case if you want to try them out or get them fixed. The pickups measure 6.73 Ω (Jazz) and 11.07 Ω (Precision). It comes in a generic hardshell case which has some wear and tear. All of the latches, hinges and handle are in excellent working order and the interior has no bad odors.

Actual weight for the guitar is 10 lbs 3 oz.

Please note that I do not set up my guitars before mailing. I have played it and everything is good as far as I'm concerned, but you may need to change the strings, adjust the action or do other maintenance to get it to your preferences.


I list some of my items on both eBay and Reverb. In the event that this item sells on both marketplaces before I can take the other listing down, I will honor the first sale and refund the second. There will be no exceptions to this!


Payment

Payment must be received within 4 days after I send the invoice. I am willing to work with you if you need more time, but I will cancel the order if I do not hear from you within 4 days! You will also be permanently blocked from purchasing my items in the future.

Combined Shipping

I can't combine shipping for more than one full sized guitar but can if you purchase smaller items such as parts or accessories. I can't quote exact rates until you purchase the items and I package them. So please wait for me to send a combined invoice if you buy more than one item. Local pickup is available for any item.

Offers & Package Deals

Please note that I do not consider offers! I will not end any listing early so please do not ask! Please don't ask for package deals on multiple items and I will not do any deals off of eBay. I will not respond to any questions asking for any of the above!

P.O. Boxes

I have blocked P.O. Boxes from this listing. FedEx and UPS do not ship to these addresses and USPS shipping is much more costly. I will ship to a P.O. Box if you contact me ahead of time and agree to pay for USPS instead of FedEx or UPS.

UPS Signature – Update Your Address!

Please note that you or someone at your address will have to sign for this guitar. If think you will not be home, you will need to change your address BEFORE you purchase it to where you will be (work, UPS Store, etc.) I mail my items through Reverb and it goes under their UPS account, so it is impossible to change the delivery address after I mail it. I will not refund any shipping cost that I’ve already paid if the item is sent back to me because you did not sign for it! So please plan ahead so you receive your item.

item.title: ULTRA RARE 1970's Aria Pro II LB-650 ... SET NECK version (Bill Lawrence Gibson Grabber Humbuckers / 4 Way Gibson Ripper L-9S Rotary Switch / ...) + GIG BAG — $1,577.11
item.description:
$1,577.11 · Good · Vanborn Vintage Guitars

Vanborn Vintage Guitars presents …


The late 1970s Aria Pro II LB-650 is an exceptional bass from Japan’s celebrated "Lawsuit" era, revered as a stunning alternative to the Gibson L9-S Ripper.


This rare set-neck version, highly sought after for its superior sustain and tonal depth compared to bolt-on models, is equipped with exact replicas of the Bill Lawrence low-output humbuckers found in the original Ripper.


These pickups, paired with the 4-way rotary selector offering parallel/series switching and the iconic "choke" circuit, provide a versatile range of tones, making it the perfect bass for any style or genre.


This LB-650 features a beautiful sunburst finish with natural lacquer checking and aged hardware that exudes vintage character. The maple neck and rosewood fingerboard ensure warmth and clarity, while the double-cutaway body provides effortless access to upper frets.


Exclusively built for the Japan market ... at a time when they were giving the USA built Gibson version a run for its money ... guess why they are called "lawsuit" guitars.


Currently strung with broken-in flatwound strings that feel like your favorite pair of jeans, this bass delivers a smooth, warm tone perfect for the studio or stage.


Famous Gibson Ripper players like Krist Novoselic (Nirvana), Gene Simmons (KISS) (See the attached video) and Rick Danko (The Band) helped define this iconic tone, and the LB-650 offers a faithful homage to their sound.


Weight: 4.3 kg (or 9 lb and 7.678 oz)


Complete with a deluxe gig bag with good padding, this bass is ready to inspire new generations.


Features:

  • Rare set-neck design for enhanced sustain and tonal richness.
  • Bill Lawrence low-output humbucker replicas for vintage sound.
  • 4-way rotary selector with parallel/series switching and choke circuit.
  • Gorgeous sunburst finish with natural lacquer checking and aged hardware.
  • Strung with broken-in flatwound strings for a smooth, warm tone.
  • Includes a gig bag for safe storage and transport.
  • Modeled after the Gibson L9-S Ripper played by legends like Krist Novoselic, Gene Simmons, and Rick Danko.



Available for WORLDWIDE shipping with UPS, the shipping cost is calculated automatically in the shopping cart, based on your location. If you're purchasing from inside the EU, your purchase will include VAT.


Check out our Reverb feedback rating, we are known for professional safe packaging and fast shipping! Please read our shop policy on used & vintage items we have for sale and send a message if you have any questions, for more pictures or offers!


Cheers!


Vanborn Vintage Guitars

Belgium’s finest vintage & rare guitar store!

Family owned & operated in Sint-Truiden (Est. 2024)

item.title: 【used】Fender Japan / PB62-78US OLB 2006~2007 4.11kg #R007368 Crafted in Japan【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,453
item.description:
$1,453 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

It's loved by countless top artists, including James Jamerson, Bobby Vega, and Michael League. (Titles omitted)

The Precision Bass, the world's first fretted solid-body electric bass, instantly captivated bassists worldwide.

Its brilliance remains unchanged and beloved even more than half a century after its creation.

Fender Japan started in the 1980s as a Japanese-made Fender brand, but unfortunately, it has now been discontinued and integrated into Fender itself.

However, the brand's reliable quality is still supported by many artists and continues to be in demand in the market.

This is the "PB62-78US," a top-of-the-line model from that era.

The body is made of alder, and the fingerboard is rosewood.

This is a classic combination that produces the rich midrange and solid bottom end characteristic of the Precision Bass.

The body color is a beautiful, deep, and vibrant Old Lake Placid Blue (OLB).

This higher-end model features VINTAGE PU (USA) pickups, utilizing genuine USA-made parts.

The bridge is the classic upgrade part, the BADASS II.

The sound is unmistakably Precision Bass-like, characterized by a thick, gritty low-to-low-mid range.

You can experience the pure, classic Fender tone that solidly supports the foundation of any ensemble.

Both in terms of looks and sound, this is a high-quality instrument that embodies the meticulous craftsmanship of the time, making it ready to play immediately.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.

The truss rod is stiff, but approximately 90 degrees of movement has been confirmed.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have scattered dents throughout. There is also some tarnishing on the metal parts.

The electronics are in good condition.

The bridge has been replaced with a BADASS BASS II.

Controls: Volume, Tone
Nut Width: 41mm
Bridge String Spacing: 20mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 2006-2007
Country of Manufacture: JAPAN

During setup, we use SIT Strings, favored by many professional artists.

They are characterized by stable tuning and a powerful, three-dimensional sound. Please enjoy them as well.

item.title: Sterling by Music Man SB4 Candy Apple Red- Used — $289
item.description:
$289 · Very Good · Mill River Music & Guitar Shop

Sterling by Music Man SB4, Candy Apple Red - Used
The SB4 is Sterling by Music Man's take on the classic Music Man Sterling body shape, brought into the SUB Series at an accessible price point. It carries the essential DNA — contoured offset double-cutaway body, single humbucking pickup, and a 2-band active preamp that gives you real tonal flexibility beyond what a passive bass at this price typically offers. The shallower neck profile makes it easy to get your hand around, and it plays solidly across the board with comfortable action sitting right at spec. No visible fret or nut wear anywhere. A ding on the front and minor scuffing and buckle rash on the back — see photos for detail — but nothing that affects playability. A good entry-level bass for players ready to step up from the basics, with the active electronics to grow into.
- Make: Sterling by Music Man
- Model: SB4

- Body: Soft maple

About Mill River Music Mill River Music is an independent, human-powered music shop located in downtown Northampton, MA, run by a team of players, enthusiasts, and certified repair technicians. As Martin and Taylor certified techs, we hold our guitar work to a high standard, and we're committed to full transparency about the condition of everything we sell. No surprises.
When you buy from Mill River, you're supporting an independent local business that genuinely cares — about gear, about players, and about doing things right. We're here every step of the way. Reach out anytime.

$4,081 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Roscoe is a high-end bass brand based in North Carolina, USA.
It's known for being used by musicians like Jimmy Haslip, Anthony Crawford, and in Japan, Wataru Suzuki.

This model is a somewhat rare Century 6-string model.

The horn is designed to be slightly longer, and the exquisite body balance makes it easy for those accustomed to Fender styles to switch to it without feeling any discomfort.

The body top is made of beautiful quilted maple, the back is made of ash, and the fingerboard is made of high-quality ebony.

It features a combination of hard, high-quality wood materials that provide excellent attack.

The pickups are Bartolini soapbar type.

They output a rich, full-bodied midrange that is pleasing to the ear.

The preamp is the legendary Aguilar OBP-1.

The intuitive 2-band EQ (Bass/Treble) allows for smooth addition of dynamic thickness and sound pressure.

The sound is based on a crisp, wide-range character, with a rich mid-low range that blends exquisitely.

While fully utilizing the wide range of the 6 strings and 24 frets, the nuance of uniform and clear output across all frequencies from Low-B to the highest register is a rare characteristic unique to this brand.

This is a supreme instrument that combines the overwhelming playability expected of a high-end bass with a superb, practical tone.

The frets have approximately 90% remaining.

The truss rod is stiff, but approximately 90 degrees of movement has been confirmed.

item.title: 【new】Aria Pro II / SB-CLB - Cliff Burton Signature 4,550kg #WI26040115【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $3,144
item.description:
$3,144 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Aria Pro II is an international brand that has been active since the early days of guitar making in Japan.


This model is the SB, a flagship model of Aria Pro II.


One of the greatest masterpieces in the history of Japanese guitars has made a comeback.


The SB has been loved by musicians all over the world.


Marcus Miller, Will Lee, Jack Bruce, and John Taylor are known bassists who have used the SB. (Titles omitted)


Currently, the latest model, the SB-1000, is being manufactured.


This model is a signature model of Cliff Burton, the bassist of Metallica.


He was a big fan of this SB series.


Its iconic look and sound will be reproduced in a limited production run in 2026.


It features a neck-through construction, with a 7-piece laminated neck made of maple and walnut.


The beauty of the wood grain, which runs smoothly from the tip of the headstock to the bottom of the bridge, is exceptional.


The fingerboard is rosewood. The standard model uses ebony.


The body is made of ash.


The body shape is designed with weight balance in mind; in fact, the bridge side of the body is thicker than the headstock side.


This shifts the weight balance towards the bridge, resulting in a comfortable balance during playing.


The color is, of course, all black.


The tuning machines are GOTOH GB707, and the bridge is a QHB-1BR.


While the standard model's bridge is also made by GOTOH, this likely recreates the look of the original.


This shows that this is not just a limited edition model with a different color, but a project made with love.


The pickups are dual-coil MB-10E.


The preamp is the familiar B.B. Circuit.


This circuit, which has captivated many bassists, is an active low-pass filter that allows you to freely switch between six different characteristics.


From a warm and thick sound to a bright and attacky sound, the character changes with the switch. Simple yet highly practical, it allows for quick and optimal sound shaping to suit playing style and ensemble.


This filter can be switched on and off with a mini-switch.


Controls include volume, tone, mode selector, and B.B. Circuit bypass switch.


It features a thick, attack-oriented sound with a tight yet smoothly extending sustain.


This is Cliff Burton's SB.


SPECS


Body: Ash

Neck: Maple + Walnut, Neck-Through body, Heel-Less Cutaway

Fingerboard: Rosewood

Frets: 24F

Scale: 864mm

Pickups: MB-10E Dual Coil

Controls: Volume, Tone, 6-position PU Attack Mode Selector SW, Bypass SW, Battery LED

Bridge: QHB-1BR

Hardware: Chrome

Finish: BK (Black)


Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.


Accessories: Gig bag, poster, warranty card

With the exception of some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.


The warranty period is 3 months for used products and 1 year for new products.


Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:


* Malfunctions and damage caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.


* Replacement of consumables.


* Malfunctions and damage caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.


* Malfunctions and damage caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual.


* Malfunctions or damage to parts repaired or modified by a party other than our company.


* If the warranty certificate does not have your name, model name, serial number, etc. filled in, or if the wording has been altered.


* If the warranty certificate is not presented or is lost.


(Warranty is only provided for purchases made in Japan.)


● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in


When you use our trade-in service at the time of purchase, the sales price will be 10% off. (Consignment items are excluded. This offer is only applicable if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the selling price.)


Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.


We will ship with sufficient packaging.


We also ship internationally.



●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee

Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.


If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.


Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.

Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

The Fender electric bass is a quintessential example of a classic bass. The Player Precision Bass, inheriting the true Fender style, boasts a heavy, growling sound that has inspired countless copy models. With its smooth playability and spotlight-worthy style, this bass delivers a thunderous sound, making it a valuable partner in both studio and stage settings, helping players expand their creative vision to the world.

The Player series, which respects tradition while maintaining an innovative spirit, features a split-coil Precision Bass middle pickup that boosts volume to suit modern ears, delivering the fat, punchy, legendary Fender bass tone. The master volume and tone controls, which adjust the powerful sound through the pickup, are positioned within easy reach for easy sound shaping. The modern C-shaped maple neck is paired with a 9.5-inch radius fingerboard with 20 medium jumbo frets, accommodating a variety of playing styles. Furthermore, the 4-saddle bridge provides accurate intonation and is designed to be easily adjusted to suit your playing style and taste.

There is some tarnishing and rust on the parts, as well as minor scratches and light dents, but it is in relatively good condition.
The truss rod has plenty of adjustment range, and although there is a slight twist in the neck, it is currently playable without any issues.

Weight ≈ 4.20 kg
Accessories: General-purpose soft case

item.title: Atelier Z M#245 / 70' LTD -Vintage Natural- #201154[4.16kg][Made in Japan] — $2,503.31
item.description:
$2,503.31 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

-----

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.

-----

Welcome to

Kurosawa Ikebukuro! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our

shop policy below the guitar description!

For Paypal payments, please register your address in your Paypal account in advance.

------------------------------------------

Many of you have likely been waiting for this!

We are thrilled to announce the arrival of the "70s-spec" model—a version that had unfortunately been discontinued but is now back by popular demand.

Its stylish design, featuring a fretboard devoid of position markers, is a special touch rarely seen elsewhere.


Since its establishment in 1989, ATELIER Z has continuously developed instruments in collaboration with top-tier bassists—such as the late Tomohito Aoki—creating numerous masterpieces through a unique approach that incorporates professional setups and active circuitry.

The solid sound, crafted by integrating feedback from professional bassists, has garnered support from a wide range of users, from professionals to amateurs.


This instrument is based on the "M Series"—Atelier Z's flagship line that has enjoyed enduring popularity for nearly three decades since its debut—and is specifically configured to the "70s model" specifications.


It features specs that epitomize the 70s aesthetic and tone: a rear pickup positioned 1cm closer to the bridge, a black pickguard, a metal control plate, and a "Vintage Natural" finish.


The tight grain of the ash body is reflected in the sound; it delivers a classic "scooped" (or "smile-curve") tone—solid, thick, and grounded in the fundamental notes, yet with sparkling, sharp, and punchy high-end clarity.

This instrument offers a uniquely exhilarating feel when playing slap bass—an experience only possible with these specific specs.


The rear pickup's proximity to the bridge also ensures the Low-B string rings out with exceptional clarity—a distinct advantage of the 70s style.


Crafted with the high precision characteristic of Japanese manufacturing, it allows for a low-action setup with outstanding playability, accommodating various techniques such as fingerstyle, picking, and slapping.


It is equipped with original pickups and the classic Bartolini XTCT preamp. The XTCT features "Spectrum Boost" wiring, tuned to deliver higher sound pressure than the standard XTCT model.

With a 2-band EQ that allows for switching to passive mode, it offers a wide range of tonal possibilities. We invite you to experience the "Atelier Z sound" for yourself.

We look forward to hearing from you.


~Specifications~

Scale: 34"

Neck: 1-Piece Maple

Fingerboard: Maple (20 Frets)

Body: 2-Piece Ash

Tuners: GOTOH GB528

Bridge: ATELIER Z BB419

Pickups: ATELIER Z JBZ-4 (Rear: '70s position)

Preamp: Bartolini XTCT + Spectrum Boost

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Tone (Active ON/OFF), Treble/Bass

Weight: Approx. 4.16 kg

Included accessories: Gig bag

item.title: Dynelectron Longhorn Bass 1960´s - Copperburst — $1,298.84
item.description:
$1,298.84 · Good · J´s Guitar Shop

Dieser Vintage  Dynelectron Longhorn Bass (Shortscale) ist ein toller Player, Bespielbarkeit Super, der Sound knurrig, trocken….mit jeder Menge Vintage Mojo

Hier zum fairen Kurs….

Nach meinen Recherchen wurde der Bass ca. 1965 von Meazzi in Italien gebaut der nachdem Danelectro pleite war die Produktion weiterführte.

Der Bass ist technisch im guten Zustand, ist neu besaitet und hat ein J´s Guitar Shop Setup bekommen.

Optisch gibt es viele Blessuren, aber nichts wildes…siehe Fotos.

Ein Tuner ist nicht Original, das Pickguard fehlt.

Da die Originale Brücke leider nicht vorhanden war, habe ich eine Allparts Danelectro Brücke verbaut, welche alleine durch die Einstellmöglichkeiten der Intonation eine Verbesserung darstellt.

Schraubenlöcher im Bereich unter und neben der Bücke wurden fachmännisch verdübelt/geschlossen

Die Herstellerfirma existiert nicht mehr, oder über den Hersteller ist nichts herauszufinden

item.title: 【used】Wood Custom Guitars / Throb-5 4.830kg #153【Consignment】【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $4,968
item.description:
$4,968 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Wood Custom Guitars, a new Japanese brand bearing a dignified name.

Their dedication to wood is clearly reflected in their quality, and their reputation has rapidly grown in recent years.

Artists known to use this brand include Sohei Mishima (Cinema Staff), Itsuki Shinohara (ex Enma., Karanoa…), Aribobo (Yabai T-shirts-ya), and Hiromi Hirohiro (tricot).

This is the Throb-5, a 5-string model adopting a modern JB style.

The body top is made of Bogwood, and the body back is made of Walnut.

The neck is made of 3-piece Maple, and the fingerboard is made of Ebony.

The unique grain of the Bogwood and the deep black Ebony fingerboard give it a luxurious and impressive finish.

It is equipped with Nordstrand DC-5 pickups and a Trickfish FlexCore preamp.

While it boasts a very modern design, the sound retains the powerful bass and crisp treble characteristic of a JB bass.

Yet, the texture is smooth, well-balanced, and refined.

This is a masterpiece that blends traditional and high-end textures in a modern way.

The frets have approximately 95% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

There are screw holes on the headstock of the body and paintwork. Overall, it is in near-mint condition.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Passive Tone, Bass, High-Mid/Low-Mid, Treble, Preamp On/Off Switch, Series/Parallel Switch x2, Preamp Gain (in cavity)
Nut Width: 46.8mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: Unknown
Country of Manufacture: JAPAN

item.title: 【used】Wood Custom Guitars / Vibe-4 #164 - BPDF Sparkle 2023 4.225kg #164【Consignment】【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $4,039
item.description:
$4,039 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Wood Custom Guitars, a new Japanese brand bearing a dignified name,

its dedication to wood is clearly reflected in its quality, and its reputation has rapidly grown in recent years.

Artists known to use this brand include Sohei Mishima (Cinema Staff), Itsuki Shinohara (Karanoa), Aribobo (Yabai T-Shirt Ya-san), and Hiromi Hirohiro (tricot).

This is the brand's flagship model, the Vibe-4.

While based on the traditional Jazz Bass style, it features a design that incorporates the needs of modern players.

The body is made of alder, the neck of maple, and the fingerboard of Indian rosewood.

This is a classic wood combination that provides a rich midrange and well-balanced response.

What immediately catches the eye is the beautiful BPDF Sparkle finish.

This coloring, done by SAITO COLOUR WORKS, creates a striking look that changes its appearance depending on the viewing angle.

This guitar features Evertone pickups.

The preamp has been replaced with a Bartolini XTCT/MCT-500, and a KarDian Trehalose has also been added. The potentiometers and wiring have been replaced accordingly.

The sound boasts a modern character with a well-balanced tight low end, a prominent midrange, and extended treble.

While maintaining the traditional JB nuances, it delivers a high-fidelity, wide-range sound, offering excellent expressiveness across a wide range of genres.

The frets have approximately 95% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and finish are generally clean.

The electronics are in good condition.

The preamp has been replaced with a Bartolini XTCT/MCT-500. The potentiometers and wiring have been replaced accordingly. A KarDian Phase Inversion is installed.

Controls: Neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, master tone, bass, middle, treble, active/passive switch
Nut width: 38.5mm
String spacing at bridge: 19.0mm
Scale: 34 inches
Year of manufacture: 2023
Country of manufacture: JAPAN

This instrument will be delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

This is a consignment item, so shipping costs may differ from the usual.

item.title: G&L Tribute JB-2 4 String Electric Bass Guitar - Shell Pink — $599
item.description:
$599 · B-Stock · Rayhill Audio

Bassists who are fond of the old adage "if it ain’t broke, don't fix it" clearly haven’t played a G&L JB-2! This rock-solid workhorse instrument from G&L's affordable Tribute Series is all about refinement — taking the iconic J-bass formula and refining it by blending "holy grail" Alnico single-coil tone with modern Leo Fender-designed innovations.

For starters, the JB-2 has a more contemporary "vibe" with its comfortable body shape inspired by G&L's L Series models. The slim Maple neck features a smooth satin finish, while the relatively flat 12" radius and medium-jumbo nickel frets deliver modern playability. What's more, the JB-2 boasts G&L 's acclaimed 'Saddle-Lock' bridge that transfers string energy right into the body for incredible resonance!

Features

Body Shape: JB-2
Body Wood: Poplar
Neck Wood: Hard Rock Maple
Neck Profile: Medium "C"
Neck Finish: Clear Tint Satin
Neck Joint: Bolt-On
Scale Length: 34"
Fingerboard Wood: Maple
Fingerboard Radius: 12"
Fret Size: Medium Jumbo, Nickel
Number of Frets: 21
Inlays: Black Dots
Nut Width: 1.5"
Bridge: Leo Fender-Designed G&L Saddle-Lock
Tuners: Traditional Open-Back
Hardware Finish: Chrome
Pickup: G&L Alnico V Single-Coils
Controls: Volume (x2), Master Tone
Case: Sold Separately 

Weight: 9 lb 2 oz



Condition:

This is an NOS (new old stock) Electric Guitar from G&L. It's from the remaining inventory from the G&L warehouse. It's a bran new never played guitar that is being sold as used without warranty. We found a tiny mark on the top next to the volume knob. The actual guitar for sale is shown in the photographs. 

Shipping:

Guaranteed free, fast and safe UPS shipping to lower 48 states. International UPS expedited and USPS Priority shipping available to most countries. We ship our items the same or next business day. All items are professionally packed for safe transit. 


Returns:

We back every guitar we sell with a 14 day return policy. If you do not like the way it feels, plays, looks or sounds upon arrival, just let us know and you can return it. See our shop policy link for full details.

item.description:
$749 · B-Stock · Rayhill Audio

The Tribute Series Fallout Bass is available in four exciting colors with complimenting pickguards. This 30” short scale powerhouse features a G&L Magnetic Field Design humbucking pickup controlled by a 3-position mini–toggle switch for parallel/split/OMG modes – just like an L•1000 – bringing anything from modern to old school tones to your fingertips. What’s more, players used to a traditional 34″ scale will find this pint size puncher super easy to play.

Other features include a Poplar body, Maple bolt-on neck, Rosewood and Maple fingerboards, Saddle-Lock Bridge, G&L tuners, and D’Addario Nickel Roundwound short scale strings, .045-.065-.080-.100.

item.title: 【used】Fender Japan / PB57-53 Tobacco Sunburst 1994-1995 3.910kg #S030135【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,171
item.description:
$1,171 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

It's loved by countless top artists, including Jaco Pastorious, Marcus Miller, and Geddy Lee. (Titles omitted)

The Precision Bass, introduced in 1951 as the world's first fretted solid-body electric bass, has been loved by bassists worldwide for over half a century.

Fender Japan is a discontinued brand, but many artists still use their instruments, and its high quality is still highly regarded.

This model is a PB57, faithfully reproducing the style of the 1957 PB.

Based on the specifications listed in the original catalog (the pickups are not USA-made, and the bridge is not spiral), we've determined it to be a PB57-53.

The body is made of basswood. The neck and fingerboard are 1-piece maple.

The pickups are original.

The strap pins and anodized pickguard have been replaced with Schaller parts.

Additionally, the cavity is shielded with silver foil tape.

This bass features the characteristic thick low end and open midrange of a Precision Bass.

With its unchanging standard specifications, it fits all genres, including R&B, rock, and pop.

The frets have approximately 70% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork show overall scratches and small dents. Scratches are also visible on the pickup covers. Some tarnishing is visible on the metal parts.

The electronics are in good condition.

The strap pins have been replaced with Schaller ones. The pickguard has been replaced with an anodized one. The cavity is shielded with silver foil tape.

Controls: Volume, Tone
Nut Width: 42.3mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 1994-1995
Country of Manufacture: JAPAN

Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: Original soft case (shoulder strap missing)

item.title: Fender Vintera II '60s Bass VI with Rosewood Fretboard 2023 - Lake Placid Blue — $1,318.51
item.description:
$1,318.51 · Excellent · The Guitar Bazaar by Leroux Music

Leroux Music

2114A 14 st NW

Calgary

Open Monday to Saturday


Super minty Vintera II Bass VI from 2023.

Only a tiny little mark on the back of the headstock, other than that - insanely clean and well cared for.


Comes with gig bag


Description

Revive the timeless sound of the '60s with the Vintera® II 60s Bass VI and experience the iconic looks, inspiring feel and incomparable tone that only a Fender can deliver.


The Vintera® II 60s Bass VI features an alder body and a maple neck for classic Fender tone thats full of punch and clarity. The mid-'60s "C"-shape neck lends a comfortably familiar grip that feels just right in your hand, while the 7.25" radius fingerboard with vintage-tall frets provide vintage comfort with ample room for big bends and expressive vibrato. Under the hood, you'll find a trio of vintage-style early-'60s single-coil pickups that deliver all the sweet and sparkling, warm and woody tone that made Fender famous. The vintage-style floating tremolo lets you dive and wail with abandon, while vintage-style tuning machines provide classic looks with a finer gear ratio and enhanced tuning stability to complete the package.


Experience the unmistakable vintage feel and unmatched sound of a classic Fender with the Vintera® II 60s Bass VI today and start making music history!


Features

• Alder Body

• 7.25" Radius Rosewood Fingerboard with Vintage Tall Frets

• Mid-'60s "C"-Shape Neck

• Vintage-Style Early-'60s Pickups

• Vintage-Style Floating Tremolo with Push-In Tremolo Arm

• Vintage-Style Tuning Machines


Specs

Body: Alder

Body Finish: Gloss polyester

Body Shape: Bass VI

Neck: Maple

Neck Finish: Gloss urethane

Neck Shape: Mid '60s "C"

Neck Construction: 4-Bolt

Scale Length: 30" (762 mm)

Fingerboard: Slab Rosewood with White Dot Inlay

Fingerboard Radius: 7.25" (184.1 mm)

Frets: 21

String Nut: Synthetic bone, 1.65" (42 mm) nut width

Bridge Pickup: Vintage-Style Bass VI Single-Coil

Middle Pickup: Vintage-Style Bass VI Single-Coil

Neck Pickup: Vintage-Style Bass VI Single-Coil

Controls: Master Volume, Master Tone

Pickup Switching: 3 Pickup On/Off Switches

Pickup Configuration: SSS

Bridge: 6-Saddle Vintage-Style Adjustable with "Floating" Tremolo Tailpiece

Tremolo Arm: Push-In

Hardware Finish: Nickel/Chrome

Tuning Machines: Fender® Vintage-style

Strings: Fender® USA Nickel Plated Steel (.025-.095 Gauges)

Case: Deluxe Gig Bag included

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass Deluxe 4 Active 2005 - Amber Quilt — $1,121.13
item.description:
$1,121.13 · Excellent · Band Aid Music

Fender Deluxe Jazz Bass 24 – Rare Amber Quilt Top – Korea – Seymour Duncans

The "Modern Classic" Jazz Bass only 3.9kg

Up for grabs is a stunning 2005 Fender Deluxe Jazz Bass 24 in a vibrant Cherry Sunburst finish. This isn't your average off-the-shelf Jazz Bass; this was a short-run, premium Korean export designed to compete with high-end boutique brands.

If you’re looking for that classic Fender vibe but need the extended range of 24 frets and the punch of an active preamp, this is the unicorn you've been looking for.

The Hardware & Tone

• The Look: A high-grade Quilted Maple top that pops under stage lights. No pickguard to hide the wood grain—just pure aesthetic.

• The Electronics: Factory-loaded with Seymour Duncan Basslines pickups. These are dead quiet and high-output.

• The Preamp: Includes a 4 band active EQ and an active/passive switch (mini-toggle).

• The Build: 34” scale Maple neck with a fast Rosewood board. High-mass bridge for better sustain than the stamped-metal vintage style.

Condition & Value

This bass (S/N 05049413) is in fantastic shape. The Korean-made Deluxe series is famous among bassists for having build quality that often puts contemporary Fender lines to shame. It’s solid, stays in tune thanks to the Hipshot-licensed tuners, and looks like a million bucks.

item.title: 【used】momose / MJ2-STD/M NA 3.65kg #2220【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,733
item.description:
$1,733 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This product has been leveled using PLEK.

The string height at the 12th fret will be 1.5mm for the G string and 2.0mm for the E string (2.3mm for the Low-B string) when shipped.

Momose, based in Matsumoto, Nagano, is one of Japan's leading brands producing high-quality basses and guitars.

It is renowned for its beautiful appearance, superior sound, and meticulous woodworking techniques.

It enjoys the trust of professionals and is known to be used by top musicians such as Kazuki Isogai and Kyohei Ariga. (Titles omitted)

This MJ2 series is an ambitious work that incorporates Momose's unique design and interpretation into the details, based on the traditional JB style.

The body is made of two-piece ash, and the neck and fingerboard are made of maple, a classic construction.

The bridge is a reliable GOTOH 203B-4, and the tuning machines are also GOTOH GB11WC.

There's no compromise in the selection of hardware.

The pickups are Momose original VJ-1s.

This instrument has been modified from the standard two-volume control setup to a "master volume, balancer, and master tone" configuration.

This allows for instant volume adjustment and intuitive, seamless pickup blending.

The sound is appealingly balanced, with a tight, focused low end and a trebly high end with a strong attack.

The crisp, responsive feel during slap techniques is also noteworthy.

Furthermore, the ultra-smooth playability, characteristic of Deviser products, minimizes player stress, which is a significant advantage for musicians who require long playing sessions.

Combined with the ease of use of the controls, this is an instrument we confidently recommend to anyone looking for a high-quality, practical passive JB.

The frets have approximately 70% remaining.

The truss rod is stiff, but approximately 90-degree movement has been confirmed.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have numerous dents overall.

The electronics are in good condition.

The neck pickup volume pot has been replaced with a CTS balancer pot. The capacitor has also been replaced (0.047μF).

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Master Tone
Nut Width: 35.3mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: Unknown
Country of Manufacture: Japan

During setup, SIT Strings, favored by many professional artists, were used.

They are characterized by stable tuning and a powerful, three-dimensional sound. Please enjoy them.

GIB's craftsmen will adjust the guitar for easy playability before delivery.

Accessories: Hard case

Text: Kaigo Kawami

Inspector: Kawami

Product photos: Lee
